Mobile Developer.q: What is your experience with native vs cross-platform mobile development?
Mobile Developer.tip: Compare React Native, Flutter, Swift/Kotlin. Discuss trade-offs in performance, developer experience, code sharing, and when to choose each approach.

Mobile Developer.q: How do you handle different screen sizes and device fragmentation?
Mobile Developer.tip: Discuss responsive layouts, constraint-based design, adaptive components, testing on multiple devices, and platform-specific guidelines.

Mobile Developer.q: Describe your approach to mobile app performance optimization.
Mobile Developer.tip: Mention profiling tools, memory management, lazy loading, image optimization, reducing network calls, and battery consumption.

Mobile Developer.q: How do you manage app state in a mobile application?
Mobile Developer.tip: Discuss patterns like Redux, MobX, Provider, Riverpod, or platform-native solutions. Explain when local vs global state is appropriate.

Mobile Developer.q: What is your experience with push notifications and background processing?
Mobile Developer.tip: Show knowledge of FCM, APNs, background fetch, work managers, notification channels, and handling notifications in various app states.

Mobile Developer.q: How do you handle offline functionality and data syncing?
Mobile Developer.tip: Discuss local databases (SQLite, Realm, Core Data), conflict resolution strategies, sync queues, and presenting offline state to users.

Mobile Developer.q: Describe your approach to mobile app security.
Mobile Developer.tip: Mention certificate pinning, secure storage, code obfuscation, API security, and OWASP Mobile Top 10.

Mobile Developer.q: How do you test mobile applications?
Mobile Developer.tip: Discuss unit testing, UI testing frameworks (XCTest, Espresso, Detox), device farms, beta distribution, and crash reporting.

Mobile Developer.q: What is your experience with app store submission and release management?
Mobile Developer.tip: Show knowledge of review guidelines, provisioning profiles, signing, staged rollouts, A/B testing, and handling rejections.

Mobile Developer.q: How do you handle deep linking and navigation in mobile apps?
Mobile Developer.tip: Discuss URL schemes, universal links, app links, navigation libraries, and managing complex navigation stacks.

Mobile Developer.q: Describe a time you had to optimize an app that was crashing or running slowly.
Mobile Developer.tip: Use STAR. Show profiling skills, root cause analysis, and measurable improvement in crash rates or performance metrics.

Mobile Developer.q: How do you stay current with mobile development trends and platform updates?
Mobile Developer.tip: Mention WWDC, Google I/O, blogs, podcasts, community involvement, and how you evaluate new APIs and tools.

Machine Learning Engineer.q: Describe your experience building and deploying machine learning models.
Machine Learning Engineer.tip: Walk through your pipeline: data collection, feature engineering, model selection, training, evaluation, deployment, and monitoring.

Machine Learning Engineer.q: How do you handle imbalanced datasets?
Machine Learning Engineer.tip: Discuss oversampling (SMOTE), undersampling, class weights, appropriate metrics (F1, AUC-PR), and when each technique is most effective.

Machine Learning Engineer.q: What is your approach to feature engineering?
Machine Learning Engineer.tip: Show domain knowledge, feature selection techniques, handling missing values, encoding categorical variables, and automated feature generation.

Machine Learning Engineer.q: How do you evaluate model performance beyond accuracy?
Machine Learning Engineer.tip: Discuss confusion matrices, precision, recall, F1, AUC-ROC, calibration, and choosing metrics aligned with business objectives.

Machine Learning Engineer.q: Describe your experience with deep learning frameworks.
Machine Learning Engineer.tip: Compare TensorFlow, PyTorch, JAX. Discuss model architectures you have built, training strategies, and debugging approaches.

Machine Learning Engineer.q: How do you handle model drift and monitoring in production?
Machine Learning Engineer.tip: Discuss data distribution monitoring, performance decay detection, retraining triggers, A/B testing new models, and rollback strategies.

Machine Learning Engineer.q: What is your approach to hyperparameter tuning?
Machine Learning Engineer.tip: Discuss grid search, random search, Bayesian optimization, early stopping, and how you balance computational cost with model improvement.

Machine Learning Engineer.q: How do you make models explainable to non-technical stakeholders?
Machine Learning Engineer.tip: Mention SHAP, LIME, feature importance, partial dependence plots, and translating model outputs into business narratives.

Machine Learning Engineer.q: Describe your experience with NLP or computer vision projects.
Machine Learning Engineer.tip: Show depth in at least one domain: specific architectures (transformers, CNNs), datasets, preprocessing, and results achieved.

Machine Learning Engineer.q: How do you manage ML experiment tracking and reproducibility?
Machine Learning Engineer.tip: Discuss MLflow, Weights & Biases, version control for data and models, random seeds, and documenting experiment configurations.

Machine Learning Engineer.q: What is your experience with deploying models at scale?
Machine Learning Engineer.tip: Discuss model serving (TF Serving, TorchServe), containerization, latency optimization, batching, and infrastructure choices.

Machine Learning Engineer.q: How do you ensure fairness and reduce bias in your models?
Machine Learning Engineer.tip: Discuss bias detection, fairness metrics, representative training data, adversarial debiasing, and organizational accountability.

QA Engineer.q: How do you design a comprehensive test strategy for a new feature?
QA Engineer.tip: Discuss risk-based testing, test levels (unit, integration, E2E), test data management, and aligning test scope with requirements.

QA Engineer.q: Describe your experience with test automation frameworks.
QA Engineer.tip: Be specific about tools (Selenium, Cypress, Playwright, Appium), architecture patterns (Page Object), and maintenance strategies.

QA Engineer.q: How do you decide what to automate vs test manually?
QA Engineer.tip: Show judgment: automate repetitive regression and data-driven tests; manually test exploratory, usability, and one-time scenarios.

QA Engineer.q: What is your approach to API testing?
QA Engineer.tip: Discuss tools (Postman, REST Assured), contract testing, schema validation, error handling, and performance testing at the API level.

QA Engineer.q: How do you handle flaky tests?
QA Engineer.tip: Discuss root cause analysis, common causes (timing, data dependencies, environment), quarantine strategies, and fixing vs removing flaky tests.

QA Engineer.q: Describe your experience with performance and load testing.
QA Engineer.tip: Mention tools (JMeter, Gatling, k6), defining SLAs, identifying bottlenecks, and communicating results to development teams.

QA Engineer.q: How do you ensure test coverage is adequate?
QA Engineer.tip: Discuss code coverage tools, requirement traceability matrices, risk coverage, mutation testing, and the difference between coverage and quality.

QA Engineer.q: What is your approach to testing in CI/CD pipelines?
QA Engineer.tip: Discuss test pyramid, parallel execution, fast feedback loops, test tagging, and balancing speed with thoroughness.

QA Engineer.q: How do you handle testing with incomplete or changing requirements?
QA Engineer.tip: Show collaboration with product owners, exploratory testing, risk assessment, and adapting test plans as requirements evolve.

QA Engineer.q: Describe a critical bug you found and how you communicated it.
QA Engineer.tip: Use STAR. Show severity assessment, clear reproduction steps, impact analysis, and effective communication with stakeholders.

QA Engineer.q: How do you approach accessibility testing?
QA Engineer.tip: Discuss WCAG guidelines, screen reader testing, keyboard navigation, color contrast tools, and integrating accessibility into test plans.

QA Engineer.q: What metrics do you use to measure testing effectiveness?
QA Engineer.tip: Discuss defect escape rate, test coverage, mean time to detection, automation percentage, and how you use metrics to improve processes.
